message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  In this paper, we estimate the reproductive number
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                  of COVID-19 based on Wallinga and Lipsitch framework [11] and a novel statistical time delay dynamic system. We use the observed data reported in CCDC’s paper to estimate distribution of the generation interval of the infection and apply the simulation results from the time delay dynamic system as well as released data from CCDC to fit the growth rate. The conclusion is: Based our Fudan-CCDC model, the growth rate
                  <jats:italic>r</jats:italic>
                  of COVID-19 is almost in [0.30, 0.32] which is larger than the growth rate 0.1 estimated by CCDC [9], and the reproductive number
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                  of COVID-19 is estimated by 3.25 ≤
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                  ≤ 3.4 if we simply use
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  = 1 +
                  <jats:italic>r ∗ T</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>c</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  with
                  <jats:italic>T</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>
                    <jats:italic>c</jats:italic>
                  </jats:sub>
                  = 7.5, which is bigger than that of SARS. Some evolutions and predictions are listed.
                </jats:p>
title: The reproductive number R 0 of COVID-19 based on estimate of a statistical time delay dynamical system

reference.unstructured: R. M. Anderson and R. M. May . Infectious diseases of humans: dyanmics and control. Oxford, Uk: Oxford University Press.

reference.article-title: A time delay dynamical model for outbreak of 2019-nCov and the pramameter identification[EB/OL]
reference.journal-title: To appear in Journal in Inverse and Ill-posed Problems
A time delay dynamic system with external source for the local outbreak of 2019-nCoV

reference.unstructured: N. Ferguson et al. Strategies for containing an emerging influenza pandemic in Southeast Asia. Nature 437, 209–214.
N. Ferguson et al. Strategies for mitigating and influenza pandemic. Nature 442, 448–452.

K. Liu , Y. Jiang , Y. Yan , and W. Chen . A time delay dynamic model with external source and the basic reproductive number estimation for the outbreak of Novel Coronavirus Pneumonia. Submitted to Contral Theory and Applications (in Chinese), 2020.
O. G. Pybus et al. The epidemic behaviour of hepatitis C virus. Science 22, 2323–2325.

N. Shao , Y. Chen , J. Cheng and W. Chen . Some novel statistical time delay dynamic model by statistics data from CCDC on Novel Coronavirus Pneumonia, submitted to Journal of Control Theory and Applications(in Chinese), 2020.

J.T. Wu , K. Leung , G. M. Leung , Nowcasting and forecasting the potential domestic and international spread of the 2019-nCov outbreak originating in Wuhan, China: a modelling study. The Lancet., 2020, S0140-6736(20)30260-9.

reference.article-title: Modeling and prediction for the trend of outbreak of 2019-nCoV based on a time-delay dynamic system (in Chinese)

reference.unstructured: S. Zhao , Q. Lin , J. Ran , S.S. Musa , G. Yang , Y. Lou , D. Gao , L. Yang and D. He , Preliminary estimation of the basic reproduction number of novel coronaviurs(2019-nCov) in China, from 2019 to 2020: A data-driver analysis in the early phase of the outbreak. International Journal of Infectious Diseases, (92) 2020, 214–217.
